title: PM Shram Yogi Maandhan
meta_description: PM Shram Yogi Maandhan 2026 — ₹3,000/month pension for unorganized workers after 60. Monthly contribution ₹55-200, government matches equally. How to enroll.

hero.one_liner: Guaranteed ₹3,000/month pension after age 60 for unorganized sector workers — government contributes equally to your monthly deposit

what_is_it: PM Shram Yogi Maandhan (PM-SYM) is a voluntary pension scheme for unorganized sector workers launched on 15 February 2019. It guarantees a monthly pension of ₹3,000 after the worker turns 60. The unique feature is that the government matches your contribution — for every rupee you put in, the government puts an equal amount.

The monthly contribution varies by age at enrollment: ₹55/month if you join at 18 (just ₹1.8/day), increasing to ₹200/month if you join at 40. These are very small amounts — even a daily wage worker earning ₹300-500/day can comfortably contribute. The government's equal matching effectively doubles your investment.

The scheme targets workers like street vendors, domestic helpers, auto/rickshaw drivers, construction workers, rag pickers, agricultural laborers, and all other workers in the unorganized sector with monthly income up to ₹15,000. Workers should not be members of EPF, NPS (government), or ESIC.

After the subscriber turns 60, they receive ₹3,000/month pension for life. If the subscriber dies, the spouse receives 50% pension (₹1,500/month) for their lifetime. If both pass away, the accumulated corpus is returned to the nominee.

eligibility.label: Worker type
eligibility.value: Unorganized sector worker — NOT member of EPF/NPS(Govt)/ESIC

eligibility.label: Documents
eligibility.value: Aadhaar card, savings bank account/Jan Dhan account

eligibility.label: Enrollment
eligibility.value: Visit any CSC (Common Service Centre) — enrollment cannot be done online by self

extra_sections.heading: Monthly Contribution Chart
content: Your monthly contribution depends on your age at enrollment. The government contributes an equal amount:

Key insight: If you join at age 18 and contribute ₹55/month for 42 years, your total contribution = ₹27,720. You receive ₹3,000/month pension = ₹36,000/year. If you live 20 years post-60, total pension = ₹7,20,000. That's 26× your total investment. The government match and returns on the pension fund make this possible.

extra_sections.heading: PM-SYM vs Atal Pension Yojana — Which to Choose?
rows: No matching (except initial co-contribution for early joiners)

steps.title: Visit nearest CSC center
steps.description: Find CSC at locator.csccloud.in. Carry Aadhaar card, savings bank passbook (with Aadhaar linked), and mobile number. You cannot self-register online — enrollment must be done at CSC.

steps.title: CSC operator enrolls you on maandhan.in
steps.description: The operator enters your details, verifies Aadhaar via OTP/biometric, captures your photo, and registers you on the PM-SYM portal. Choose auto-debit from your bank account.

steps.title: Monthly auto-debit starts
steps.description: Your contribution is auto-debited from your savings/Jan Dhan account every month. Ensure sufficient balance. Missed payments can be paid later without penalty during the same financial year.

steps.title: Pension starts at 60
steps.description: On your 60th birthday, ₹3,000/month pension begins. Credited directly to your bank account. Continues for lifetime.

how_to_apply.title: Visit any CSC center with Aadhaar + bank passbook
how_to_apply.description: Find nearest CSC at locator.csccloud.in or ask at your Gram Panchayat office.

how_to_apply.title: Enroll through the CSC operator
how_to_apply.description: Operator verifies identity and registers you on maandhan.in portal.

how_to_apply.title: Set up monthly auto-debit
how_to_apply.description: ₹55-200/month debited from your bank account. Government matches equally.

how_to_apply.title: Receive pension from age 60
how_to_apply.description: ₹3,000/month for life. Spouse gets ₹1,500/month after subscriber's death.

important_alert.text: You can choose EITHER PM-SYM or Atal Pension Yojana — not both. If you want a pension higher than ₹3,000/month, choose APY (up to ₹5,000/month). If you want government matching contribution, choose PM-SYM.

important_dates.label: Enrollment
important_dates.value: Open throughout the year at CSC centers

faqs.question: Can I exit PM-SYM before 60?
faqs.answer: Yes, but you only get back your own contributions with bank savings interest rate — you lose the government's matching contribution and all investment returns. Exit is allowed anytime but is not recommended unless absolutely necessary.

faqs.question: I'm already in APY. Can I join PM-SYM?
faqs.answer: No. PM-SYM and APY are mutually exclusive — you can be in only one. If you're already contributing to APY, you cannot join PM-SYM and vice versa. Choose based on your needs: APY for flexibility (₹1-5K pension), PM-SYM for government matching.

faqs.question: What if I miss monthly contributions?
faqs.answer: If you miss a monthly payment, you can pay the pending amount later during the same financial year without any penalty. If contributions remain unpaid beyond 2 years, the account may be deactivated. Contact your CSC to regularize pending payments.

faqs.question: I earn more than ₹15,000/month now. Will my enrollment be cancelled?
faqs.answer: Income is self-declared at the time of enrollment and is not continuously verified. If your income increases after enrollment, your existing enrollment continues. However, if you join EPFO/ESIC through formal employment, you should technically exit PM-SYM.

faqs.question: Is the ₹3,000 pension taxable?
faqs.answer: Technically yes — pension is treated as income. But for most PM-SYM beneficiaries, ₹3,000/month (₹36,000/year) is well below the basic tax exemption limit, so effectively no tax is payable.
